verses.latin_vulgate: Ascendit qui dispergat coram te,  qui custodiat obsidionem :  contemplare viam, conforta lumbos,  robora virtutem valde.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עָלָ֥ה מֵפִ֛יץ עַל־פָּנַ֖יִךְ נָצ֣וֹר מְצוּרָ֑ה צַפֵּֽה־דֶ֙רֶךְ֙ חַזֵּ֣ק מׇתְנַ֔יִם אַמֵּ֥ץ כֹּ֖חַ מְאֹֽד׃
verses.king_james: He that dasheth in pieces is come up before thy face: keep the munition, watch the way, make thy loins strong, fortify thy power mightily.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia reddidit Dominus superbiam Jacob,  sicut superbiam Israël ;  quia vastatores dissipaverunt eos,  et propagines eorum corruperunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י שָׁ֤ב יְהֹוָה֙ אֶת־גְּא֣וֹן יַעֲקֹ֔ב כִּגְא֖וֹן יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל כִּ֤י בְקָקוּם֙ בֹּֽקְקִ֔ים וּזְמֹרֵיהֶ֖ם שִׁחֵֽתוּ׃
verses.king_james: For the Lord hath turned away the excellency of Jacob, as the excellency of Israel: for the emptiers have emptied them out, and marred their vine branches.

verses.latin_vulgate: Clypeus fortium ejus ignitus,  viri exercitus in coccineis ;  igneæ habenæ currus in die præparationis ejus,  et agitatores consopiti sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מָגֵ֨ן גִּבֹּרֵ֜יהוּ מְאׇדָּ֗ם אַנְשֵֽׁי־חַ֙יִל֙ מְתֻלָּעִ֔ים בְּאֵשׁ־פְּלָדֹ֥ת הָרֶ֖כֶב בְּי֣וֹם הֲכִינ֑וֹ וְהַבְּרֹשִׁ֖ים הׇרְעָֽלוּ׃
verses.king_james: The shield of his mighty men is made red, the valiant men are in scarlet: the chariots shall be with flaming torches in the day of his preparation, and the fir trees shall be terribly shaken.

verses.latin_vulgate: In itineribus conturbati sunt ;  quadrigæ collisæ sunt in plateis :  aspectus eorum quasi lampades,  quasi fulgura discurrentia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּחוּצוֹת֙ יִתְהוֹלְל֣וּ הָרֶ֔כֶב יִֽשְׁתַּקְשְׁק֖וּן בָּרְחֹב֑וֹת מַרְאֵיהֶן֙ כַּלַּפִּידִ֔ים כַּבְּרָקִ֖ים יְרוֹצֵֽצוּ׃
verses.king_james: The chariots shall rage in the streets, they shall justle one against another in the broad ways: they shall seem like torches, they shall run like the lightnings.

verses.latin_vulgate: Recordabitur fortium suorum ;  ruent in itineribus suis :  velociter ascendent muros ejus,  et præparabitur umbraculum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִזְכֹּר֙ אַדִּירָ֔יו יִכָּשְׁל֖וּ (בהלכותם) [בַּהֲלִיכָתָ֑ם] יְמַֽהֲרוּ֙ חֽוֹמָתָ֔הּ וְהֻכַ֖ן הַסֹּכֵֽךְ׃
verses.king_james: He shall recount his worthies: they shall stumble in their walk; they shall make haste to the wall thereof, and the defence shall be prepared.

verses.latin_vulgate: Portæ fluviorum apertæ sunt,  et templum ad solum dirutum.
verses.king_james: The gates of the rivers shall be opened, and the palace shall be dissolved.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et miles captivus abductus est,  et ancillæ ejus minabantur gementes ut columbæ,  murmurantes in cordibus suis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהֻצַּ֖ב גֻּלְּתָ֣ה הֹֽעֲלָ֑תָה וְאַמְהֹתֶ֗יהָ מְנַֽהֲגוֹת֙ כְּק֣וֹל יוֹנִ֔ים מְתֹפְפֹ֖ת עַל־לִבְבֵהֶֽן׃
verses.king_james: And Huzzab shall be led away captive, she shall be brought up, and her maids shall lead her as with the voice of doves, tabering upon their breasts.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et Ninive quasi piscina aquarum aquæ ejus ;  ipsi vero fugerunt. State, state !  et non est qui revertatur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְנִֽינְוֵ֥ה כִבְרֵכַת־מַ֖יִם מִ֣ימֵי הִ֑יא וְהֵ֣מָּה נָסִ֔ים עִמְד֥וּ עֲמֹ֖דוּ וְאֵ֥ין מַפְנֶֽה׃
verses.king_james: But Nineveh is of old like a pool of water: yet they shall flee away. Stand, stand, shall they cry; but none shall look back.

verses.latin_vulgate: Diripite argentum, diripite aurum :  et non est finis divitiarum ex omnibus vasis desiderabilibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בֹּ֥זּוּ כֶ֖סֶף בֹּ֣זּוּ זָהָ֑ב וְאֵ֥ין קֵ֙צֶה֙ לַתְּכוּנָ֔ה כָּבֹ֕ד מִכֹּ֖ל כְּלִ֥י חֶמְדָּֽה׃
verses.king_james: Take ye the spoil of silver, take the spoil of gold: for there is none end of the store and glory out of all the pleasant furniture.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dissipata est, et scissa, et dilacerata ;  et cor tabescens, et dissolutio geniculorum,  et defectio in cunctis renibus,  et facies omnium eorum sicut nigredo ollæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בּוּקָ֥ה וּמְבוּקָ֖ה וּמְבֻלָּקָ֑ה וְלֵ֨ב נָמֵ֜ס וּפִ֣ק בִּרְכַּ֗יִם וְחַלְחָלָה֙ בְּכׇל־מׇתְנַ֔יִם וּפְנֵ֥י כֻלָּ֖ם קִבְּצ֥וּ פָארֽוּר׃
verses.king_james: She is empty, and void, and waste: and the heart melteth, and the knees smite together, and much pain is in all loins, and the faces of them all gather blackness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ubi est habitaculum leonum,  et pascua catulorum leonum,  ad quam ivit leo ut ingrederetur illuc catulus leonis,  et non est qui exterreat ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַיֵּה֙ מְע֣וֹן אֲרָי֔וֹת וּמִרְעֶ֥ה ה֖וּא לַכְּפִרִ֑ים אֲשֶׁ֣ר הָלַךְ֩ אַרְיֵ֨ה לָבִ֥יא שָׁ֛ם גּ֥וּר אַרְיֵ֖ה וְאֵ֥ין מַחֲרִֽיד׃
verses.king_james: Where is the dwelling of the lions, and the feedingplace of the young lions, where the lion, even the old lion, walked, and the lion’s whelp, and none made them afraid?

verses.latin_vulgate: Leo cepit sufficienter catulis suis,  et necavit leænis suis,  et implevit præda speluncas suas,  et cubile suum rapina.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַרְיֵ֤ה טֹרֵף֙ בְּדֵ֣י גֹרוֹתָ֔יו וּמְחַנֵּ֖ק לְלִבְאֹתָ֑יו וַיְמַלֵּא־טֶ֣רֶף חֹרָ֔יו וּמְעֹנֹתָ֖יו טְרֵפָֽה׃
verses.king_james: The lion did tear in pieces enough for his whelps, and strangled for his lionesses, and filled his holes with prey, and his dens with ravin.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce ego ad te, dicit Dominus exercituum,  et succendam usque ad fumum quadrigas tuas,  et leunculos tuos comedet gladius,  et exterminabo de terra prædam tuam,  et non audietur ultra vox nuntiorum tuorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנְנִ֣י אֵלַ֗יִךְ נְאֻם֙ יְהֹוָ֣ה צְבָא֔וֹת וְהִבְעַרְתִּ֤י בֶעָשָׁן֙ רִכְבָּ֔הּ וּכְפִירַ֖יִךְ תֹּ֣אכַל חָ֑רֶב וְהִכְרַתִּ֤י מֵאֶ֙רֶץ֙ טַרְפֵּ֔ךְ וְלֹא־יִשָּׁמַ֥ע ע֖וֹד ק֥וֹל מַלְאָכֵֽכֵה׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Behold, I am against thee, saith the Lord of hosts, and I will burn her chariots in the smoke, and the sword shall devour thy young lions: and I will cut off thy prey from the earth, and the voice of thy messengers shall no more be heard.
